bullet_summary: Bahrain has said that one person was killed by shrapnel from an intercepted missile
The death of a foreign worker at Salman Industrial City, working on a boat there, marks the kingdom’s first reported fatality in the war
Bahrain, home to the US navy’s 5th fleet, said it intercepted 61 missiles and 34 attack drones launched against it
It said some shrapnel had gotten through, striking buildings and the naval base

items.title: Israel strikes Hezbollah in Lebanon after Iran-allied group launches missiles over the border
items.standfirst: Conflict spreads to Lebanon as Hezbollah targets Israel over killing of Khamenei and IDF responds with strikes on Beirut Latest updates: US-Israel war on Iran Israel carried out heavy airstrikes on the Hezbollah-controlled southern suburbs of Beirut on Monday, after the Iran-backed group launched missiles and drones towards Israel in retaliation for the killing of Iran’s Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei.Residents of Beirut were awoken by the sounds of about a dozen blasts at 3am on Monday, as Israel struck three different locations in the southern suburbs of the capital. Continue reading...
items.content: Conflict spreads to Lebanon as Hezbollah targets Israel over killing of Khamenei and IDF responds with strikes on Beirut Latest updates: US-Israel war on Iran Israel carried out heavy airstrikes on the Hezbollah-controlled southern suburbs of Beirut on Monday, after the Iran-backed group launched missiles and drones towards Israel in retaliation for the killing of Iran’s Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei.Residents of Beirut were awoken by the sounds of about a dozen blasts at 3am on Monday, as Israel struck three different locations in the southern suburbs of the capital. Continue reading...

items.title: Israel bombs Beirut after Hezbollah fires rockets in Iran war retaliation
items.standfirst: Israel has carried out heavy strikes in the southern suburbs of Lebanon’s capital Beirut.
items.content: Israel has carried out heavy strikes in the southern suburbs of Lebanon’s capital Beirut.

items.title: Oil prices rise as Iran war threatens shipping through strait of Hormuz
items.standfirst: Brent crude jumped by 13% during early trading and stock markets came under pressure as US-Israeli strikes on Iran raised fears of disruptionOil prices rose and stock markets came under pressure on Monday after intense US-Israeli strikes on Iran prompted fears of significant global economic disruption.Brent crude jumped by as much as 13% during early trading – to hit $82 per barrel, a 14-month high – as the effective closure of the strait of Hormuz, one of the most important arteries for global trade, intensified concerns over oil supplies. Continue reading...
items.content: Brent crude jumped by 13% during early trading and stock markets came under pressure as US-Israeli strikes on Iran raised fears of disruptionOil prices rose and stock markets came under pressure on Monday after intense US-Israeli strikes on Iran prompted fears of significant global economic disruption.Brent crude jumped by as much as 13% during early trading – to hit $82 per barrel, a 14-month high – as the effective closure of the strait of Hormuz, one of the most important arteries for global trade, intensified concerns over oil supplies. Continue reading...

items.title: Trump says operations will "continue until all of our objectives are achieved"

items.content: "Sadly, there will likely be more before it ends. That's the way it is. Likely be more," President Trump said after mentioning the three U.S. service members killed in the operation.

items.title: Who takes over in Iran with Khamenei dead?
items.standfirst: Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed along with about 40 other senior members of the Islamic Republic regime in the U.S.-Israeli strikes on Saturday. Matt Gutman has more on the succession plan.
items.content: Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed along with about 40 other senior members of the Islamic Republic regime in the U.S.-Israeli strikes on Saturday. Matt Gutman has more on the succession plan.

items.content: Videos show heavy bombardment of Tehran after Israel’s military said it had launched another wave of attacks on Iran.

items.title: Trump says war with Iran will continue until all "objectives are achieved"
items.standfirst: President Trump released a new statement on Iran Sunday, saying, "Combat operations continue at this time in full force, and they will continue until all of our objectives are achieved." Willie James Inman reports.
items.content: President Trump released a new statement on Iran Sunday, saying, "Combat operations continue at this time in full force, and they will continue until all of our objectives are achieved." Willie James Inman reports.

items.title: Attack on Iran threatens to explode into wider Middle East conflict
items.standfirst: As the U.S., Israel and Iran trade strikes in the Middle East, the conflict is threatening to explode into a much wider regional conflict. Iran is retaliating against U.S. allies like Kuwait, Bahrain, the United Arab Emirates and Jordan. NBC News’ Keir Simmons reports from Dubai.

items.title: U.S., Israel and Iran trade new strikes as conflict escalates
items.standfirst: Three U.S. service members have been killed in action and five more have been seriously wounded in military operations against Iran, the U.S. military said Sunday. It comes as Israel launched a new wave of strikes in Tehran as it continued to hit targets in Iran a day after the country's supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, was killed. NBC News’ Richard Engel reports from Tel Aviv.
items.content: Three U.S. service members have been killed in action and five more have been seriously wounded in military operations against Iran, the U.S. military said Sunday. It comes as Israel launched a new wave of strikes in Tehran as it continued to hit targets in Iran a day after the country's supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, was killed. NBC News’ Richard Engel reports from Tel Aviv.

items.title: Dubai influencers stunned by Iranian strikes
items.standfirst: Influencers gone to Dubai for its luxurious lifestyle are now watching, helpless and sometimes shocked, as Iran attacks the Gulf countries, retaliating against the large-scale Israeli-American air strikes operation that killed Supreme Leader Ayatollah Al Khamenei. Since Saturday, many expatriates in the United Arab Emirates have been sharing videos of plumes of smoke rising above skyscrapers.
items.content: Influencers gone to Dubai for its luxurious lifestyle are now watching, helpless and sometimes shocked, as Iran attacks the Gulf countries, retaliating against the large-scale Israeli-American air strikes operation that killed Supreme Leader Ayatollah Al Khamenei. Since Saturday, many expatriates in the United Arab Emirates have been sharing videos of plumes of smoke rising above skyscrapers.

items.title: Deadly Austin bar shooting investigated as possible terror attack
items.standfirst: The FBI is investigating a shooting that killed two people at an Austin, Texas, bar as a possible terror attack. Jason Allen reports.
items.content: The FBI is investigating a shooting that killed two people at an Austin, Texas, bar as a possible terror attack. Jason Allen reports.

items.title: Gunman kills two in Texas bar shooting, FBI probing ‘terrorism’ link
items.standfirst: Suspect fired from SUV, then got out of the vehicle and opened fire with a rifle before being killed by officers.
items.content: Suspect fired from SUV, then got out of the vehicle and opened fire with a rifle before being killed by officers.

items.title: Deadly Texas bar shooting 'potentially act of terrorism', FBI says
items.standfirst: Two people were killed and 14 injured, with the suspected gunman, identified by some media outlets, also shot dead.
items.content: Two people were killed and 14 injured, with the suspected gunman, identified by some media outlets, also shot dead.

clusters.title: UK PM Starmer says US can use UK bases for ‘defensive strikes’ on Iran
clusters.neutral_headline: UK PM Starmer says US can use UK bases for ‘defensive strikes’ on Iran
bullet_summary: Reported by Al Jazeera, Guardian World, BBC Top Stories

items.title: UK PM Starmer says US can use UK bases for ‘defensive strikes’ on Iran
items.standfirst: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer says he’s accepted a request from the US to let his country’s military bases be used.
items.content: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer says he’s accepted a request from the US to let his country’s military bases be used.

items.title: UK to allow US to use British bases for defensive strikes against Iran
items.standfirst: Keir Starmer says the UK has not been involved in the US-Israeli strikes, but warned that 200,000 British people are in countries being targeted by IranThe UK has agreed to let the US use British military bases to attack Iranian missile sites, Keir Starmer has said.The UK has so far not been involved in the US-Israeli strikes on Iran, but in a recorded statement on Sunday evening, the prime minister said that Iran’s approach was becoming more reckless and putting British lives at risk, leading to the decision to allow the US to use two of its military bases. Continue reading...
items.content: Keir Starmer says the UK has not been involved in the US-Israeli strikes, but warned that 200,000 British people are in countries being targeted by IranThe UK has agreed to let the US use British military bases to attack Iranian missile sites, Keir Starmer has said.The UK has so far not been involved in the US-Israeli strikes on Iran, but in a recorded statement on Sunday evening, the prime minister said that Iran’s approach was becoming more reckless and putting British lives at risk, leading to the decision to allow the US to use two of its military bases. Continue reading...

items.title: UK will allow US to use bases to strike Iranian missile sites, PM says
items.standfirst: The prime minister says it remains the case that the UK will "not join offensive action now".
items.content: The prime minister says it remains the case that the UK will "not join offensive action now".

clusters.title: RAF responding to suspected drone strike at UK base in Cyprus, MoD says

items.content: The BBC understands there were no casualties in the suspected strike on RAF Akrotiri.

items.title: Why did Arsenal beat Chelsea? #sports
items.standfirst: Set pieces and a last‑ditch save decided a tense London derby Arsenal took a hard‑earned three points in a match that largely hinged on two well‑worked set plays and a dramatic late intervention from their goalkeeper. The Gunners scored twice from corners, a pattern that has become a clear…

The Gunners scored twice from corners, a pattern that has become a clear…

items.title: 'Ugly' or 'beautiful'? Set-pieces prove decisive for Arsenal again
items.standfirst: Arsenal move five points clear in the Premier League title race but is their reliance on set-pieces an issue?
items.content: Arsenal move five points clear in the Premier League title race but is their reliance on set-pieces an issue?

items.title: How did Arsenal’s win over Chelsea reshape the title race? #sports
items.standfirst: Set pieces and a late save swing the standings Arsenal relied on the two areas that have defined their season — corner routines and a match saving goalkeeper — to beat Chelsea 2 1 and restore a five point cushion at the top of the Premier League. The decisive moments came from well executed set…

items.title: Allies of US in the Gulf bear brunt of Iran attacks
items.standfirst: Iran's attacks on Gulf Arab states suggest the Islamic Republic is targeting not just the US military but also civilian infrastructure.
items.content: Iran's attacks on Gulf Arab states suggest the Islamic Republic is targeting not just the US military but also civilian infrastructure.

clusters.title: What Happens If Iran Shuts Down the Strait of Hormuz?
clusters.neutral_headline: What Happens If Iran Shuts Down the Strait of Hormuz

items.title: What Happens If Iran Shuts Down the Strait of Hormuz?
items.standfirst: The Strait of Hormuz is one of the most sensitive pressure points in the global economy. Conflict in Iran could put it at risk indefinitely.
items.content: The Strait of Hormuz is one of the most sensitive pressure points in the global economy. Conflict in Iran could put it at risk indefinitely.

items.title: What will war in Iran do to the global economy?
items.standfirst: Conflict has exposed how much growth depends on energy supplies through the Strait of Hormuz
items.content: Conflict has exposed how much growth depends on energy supplies through the Strait of Hormuz
